q.en: At what latitude does Expedition 60 travel?

options.en: Sixty degrees north latitude, through the subarctic
options.zh: 北纬 60 度，穿越亚北极地区

options.en: The equator
Twenty-five degrees south according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 南纬 25 度（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The South Pole

explain.en: The book says, 'Welcome aboard for a flight around the world at 60 degrees north latitude... over the subarctic region.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'欢迎登上这趟沿北纬 60 度环游世界的航班……飞越亚北极地区。'

q.en: Why does the trip NOT include a stop at 60 degrees south latitude?
q.zh: 为什么这趟旅程不包括在南纬 60 度的停留？

options.en: Because at 60 degrees south there is no land and no airports
options.zh: 因为在南纬 60 度没有陆地，也没有机场

options.en: Because it is too hot there
Because it is against the rules
Because the book ran out of pages according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为书页不够了（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, 'this trip won't include a visit to the same latitude line in the Southern Hemisphere. That's because at 60 degrees south latitude, there are no airports—because there is no land!'
explain.zh: 书中说：'这趟旅程不会包括南半球同一条纬度线的访问。那是因为在南纬 60 度没有机场——因为那里没有陆地！'

q.zh: 在本书中，'taiga'（泰加林）指什么？

options.en: A kind of northern light
A type of Arctic ocean current
Huge evergreen forests of the inland subarctic that withstand -40°F winters
options.zh: 亚北极内陆的巨大常绿森林，能耐受零下 40 华氏度的寒冬

options.en: A festival held in midsummer according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 盛夏时举行的一个节日（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says inland areas at 60°N 'are often covered with huge evergreen forests known as taiga... must withstand temperatures as low as -40°F in the winter.'
explain.zh: 书中说，北纬 60 度的大陆内陆'常常被巨大的常绿森林覆盖，称为泰加林……必须经受住冬季低至零下 40 华氏度的气温'。

q.en: Why does Anchorage, Alaska, have a milder climate than places farther inland?
q.zh: 为什么美国阿拉斯加的安克雷奇气候比更内陆的地方温和？

options.en: It is protected by a giant wall according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它被一堵巨大的墙保护着（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: It has no mountains nearby
It is very close to the equator
It is near a large body of water (Cook Inlet), which keeps it warmer in winter
options.zh: 它靠近大片水体（库克湾），使其冬季较为温暖

explain.en: The book says, 'Because it's near water, Anchorage has a milder climate than other places farther inland. Large bodies of water keep places warmer in winter and cooler in summer.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'因为靠近水，安克雷奇的气候比更内陆的地方温和。大水体使各地冬季更暖、夏季更凉。'

q.en: About how many hours of daylight does Anchorage get on the summer solstice?
q.zh: 在夏至那天，安克雷奇大约有多少小时的日照？

options.en: Exactly twelve hours
Twenty-four hours, with no night
About nineteen hours
About five hours

explain.en: The book says, 'On that day each year, Anchorage gets about nineteen hours of daylight.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'每年的那一天，安克雷奇大约有十九小时的日照。'

q.en: What natural light display can sometimes be seen in the subarctic during winter?
q.zh: 亚北极地区在冬季有时能看到哪种自然光景？

options.en: Lightning bugs in the snow
A solar eclipse
The midday Sun at midnight according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 午夜时的正午太阳（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The aurora borealis, or northern lights

explain.en: The book says, 'you can sometimes see the aurora borealis, or northern lights,' caused by 'powerful electrical discharges high in the atmosphere.'
explain.zh: 书中说，'你有时可以看到北极光（aurora borealis）'，它是由'高层大气中强大的放电现象'造成的。

q.en: What is permafrost, and why does it make building difficult?
q.zh: 什么是永久冻土？为什么它让建造变得困难？

options.en: A warm current from the ocean
Ground that never fully thaws in the short summers, making roads and buildings hard to build
options.zh: 在短暂的夏季也从未完全消融的地下土层，使道路和建筑难以建造

options.en: A festival of lights in St. Petersburg
options.zh: 圣彼得堡的一个灯光节

options.en: A kind of frozen rain that falls in summer according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 夏季降落的一种冰冻雨（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says winters are so cold 'the ground never completely thaws during the short summers, resulting in permafrost. Permafrost makes construction of roads and buildings very difficult.'
explain.zh: 书中说，冬季极其寒冷，'在短暂的夏季里地面也从未完全解冻，形成永久冻土。永久冻土使道路和建筑的建造非常困难。'

q.en: What text features does the book include to help readers understand the places?
q.zh: 本书包含哪些帮助读者理解各地内容的版式特征？

options.en: Only a single photograph according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 仅有一张照片（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Maps, a glossary, and an index
A song lyrics page
A comic strip

explain.en: Like other books in the series, it has a table of contents, maps showing labeled locations, diagrams, a glossary, and an index.
explain.zh: 和该系列其他书一样，它包含目录、标注地点的地图、示意图、词汇表和索引。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing Expedition 60?
q.zh: 作者写《60度探险》的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To tell a fairy tale about Vikings according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 讲述一个关于维京人的童话（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To describe a summer trip around 60°N and the climate and cultures found there
options.zh: 描述一次沿北纬 60 度的夏日旅行，以及那里的气候与文化

options.en: To explain how to catch a whale
To teach how to build an igloo

explain.en: The book is a travelogue of four subarctic cities, noting their climates (long summer days, cold winters) and cultures.
explain.zh: 本书是对四座亚北极城市的游记，记录了它们的气候（夏季白昼长、冬季寒冷）和文化。

q.en: According to the book, what causes the long summer days at 60 degrees north?
q.zh: 根据本书，是什么造成了北纬 60 度夏季漫长的白昼？

options.en: The Northern Hemisphere is tilted toward the Sun in summer, extending daylight
options.zh: 夏季北半球向太阳倾斜，从而延长了白昼

options.en: The Earth stops spinning in June according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 地球在六月停止自转（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: The Moon blocks the night
The ocean reflects extra light

explain.en: The book says the warm days and long daylight happen 'because the Northern Hemisphere is tilted toward the Sun during summer, which extends the time between sunrise and sunset.'
explain.zh: 书中说，温暖的天气和漫长的白昼是因为'夏季北半球向太阳倾斜，从而延长了日出到日落之间的时间'。

prompt.en: Imagine you are visiting one of the subarctic cities in summer. What would you most like to see or do, and what would you pack for the weather?
prompt.zh: 想象你在夏季访问亚北极地区的一座城市。你最想看什么或做什么？你会为那里的天气准备带些什么？

en: Name one city from the book (Anchorage, Oslo, Helsinki, or St. Petersburg).
Explain what the weather and daylight would be like there in summer.
zh: 说出书中一座城市的名字（安克雷奇、奥斯陆、赫尔辛基或圣彼得堡）。
说明那里夏季的天气和白昼情况会是怎样。
